frames.name: Economic Impact Assessment
frames.description: Focuses on quantifying economic consequences and identifying which countries/regions will be most affected by the blockade
frames.emphasis: Specific percentage impacts, vulnerable countries (Thailand, India, Korea, Philippines), oil price projections above $100/barrel

frames.name: Market Volatility Focus
frames.description: Emphasizes immediate market reactions and price movements in oil and gas futures
frames.emphasis: Daily price changes, percentage increases in oil/gas prices, trading data and futures movements

frames.name: Geopolitical Tensions
frames.description: Frames the story as an escalating conflict with diplomatic implications
frames.emphasis: Military operations, retaliation cycles, calls for de-escalation, international diplomatic responses

frames.name: Supply Chain Disruption
frames.description: Highlights the physical interruption of global trade and shipping
frames.emphasis: Shipping route closures, vessel counts, rerouting of container ships, physical infrastructure impacts

outlierPerspectives: China's specific diplomatic call for vessel protection and navigation safety (The Guardian only)
Detailed breakdown of LNG import dependencies by specific percentages for Pakistan, Bangladesh, and India (CNBC only)
Focus on fertilizer trade disruption through the strait (The Guardian only)
missingContext: Historical precedent for Strait of Hormuz closures and their duration
Alternative shipping routes and their capacity/cost implications
Strategic petroleum reserve levels in affected countries
Timeline and specific triggers that led to the current escalation
International maritime law implications of the blockade
Military capabilities and logistics of maintaining the closure
Previous diplomatic efforts to prevent this scenario
